error_source.expected_solution: Primary timeout config in httpx: httpx/httpx/_config.py (class Timeout, DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_CONFIG = Timeout(timeout=5.0)). Cross-repo handoff in httpx: httpx/httpx/_transports/default.py (AsyncHTTPTransport.handle_async_request, map_httpcore_exceptions, HTTPCORE_EXC_MAP). Per-operation enforcement in httpcore: httpcore/httpcore/_async/connection_pool.py (AsyncConnectionPool, AsyncPoolRequest.wait_for_connection) and httpcore/httpcore/_async/http11.py (AsyncHTTP11Connection._receive_response_headers, _receive_response_body).
evaluation_criteria: Must identify httpx/httpx/_config.py as the location of the Timeout class and DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_CONFIG
Must identify httpx/httpx/_transports/default.py as the cross-repo boundary that constructs httpcore.ConnectionPool and maps exceptions
Must identify httpcore/httpcore/_async/connection_pool.py as the connection pool that consumes timeout values from the request extensions
Must identify httpcore/httpcore/_async/http11.py as the per-operation enforcement point that raises ReadTimeout
Must reference a specific class or function (Timeout, DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_CONFIG, AsyncHTTPTransport, HTTPCORE_EXC_MAP, AsyncConnectionPool, AsyncHTTP11Connection) not just the file path

error_chain.expected_solution: Timeout marshaling chain: (1) httpx.AsyncClient is constructed with a Timeout instance from httpx/_config.py whose four dimensions (connect, read, write, pool) accept a number or None -> (2) When no timeout is passed httpx falls back to DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_CONFIG = Timeout(timeout=5.0) -> (3) AsyncHTTPTransport.handle_async_request in httpx/_transports/default.py constructs an httpcore.Request and passes timeouts via the request extensions dict, NOT the ConnectionPool constructor -> (4) httpcore AsyncConnectionPool.handle_async_request reads timeout out of extensions, calls wait_for_connection(timeout=pool) and forwards read/write to AsyncHTTP11Connection -> (5) AsyncHTTP11Connection._receive_response_headers / _receive_response_body raises httpcore.ReadTimeout when the read dimension is exceeded -> (6) map_httpcore_exceptions in httpx/_transports/default.py uses HTTPCORE_EXC_MAP to re-raise as httpx.ReadTimeout.
evaluation_criteria: Must describe the source step starting at the Timeout class in httpx/_config.py
Must describe the propagation step where timeouts are passed via request extensions, not ConnectionPool constructor
Must describe the enforcement step in httpcore _async/http11.py raising httpcore.ReadTimeout
Must describe the exception remap step via map_httpcore_exceptions and HTTPCORE_EXC_MAP
Must reference the chain in causal order from httpx public API down to httpcore enforcement and back up via the exception map
Must cite at least one file from each repo in the propagation path

trigger_conditions.expected_solution: Trigger conditions: (1) An explicit Timeout(timeout=None) marshals as None for each of connect/read/write/pool and disables per-operation timeouts at the httpcore HTTP11 connection level; (2) Omitting timeout or passing a positional number falls through to DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_CONFIG = Timeout(timeout=5.0) which yields a 5 second read cap; (3) Every exception path returning to user code goes through map_httpcore_exceptions -> HTTPCORE_EXC_MAP, so even when the underlying error is httpcore.ReadTimeout the user sees httpx.ReadTimeout, which obscures the cross-repo origin.
evaluation_criteria: Must identify the condition: explicit Timeout(None) disables per-op timeouts only when each dimension is None
Must identify the condition: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_CONFIG = Timeout(timeout=5.0) is the silent default the customer is most likely hitting
Must identify the condition: HTTPCORE_EXC_MAP rewrites httpcore.ReadTimeout to httpx.ReadTimeout, hiding the cross-repo boundary in the traceback
Must explain the combined conditions necessary to reproduce the customer's reported behavior
